history.content:
why the fuck is this happening t subagents - i thought i made evryone the same main agent why the fuck is it saying it doesnt have tools - "[v2] TOOL[1]: read_file({"filename":"D:\\Prometheus\\workspace\\teams\\team_mn74d1g9_0f4d45\\workspace/x-day1-publish-pack.md"})
[v2] TOOL FAIL: File "D:\Prometheus\workspace\teams\team_mn74d1g9_0f4d45\workspace/x-day1-publish-pack.md" not found"

Yeah — this log is **not a “subagent has no tools” error**.  
It proves the opposite: the subagent **did** call `read_file`, and the tool ran.

The actual failure is just:

- path used: `D:\Prometheus\workspace\teams\team_mn74d1g9_0f4d45\workspace/x-day1-publish-pack.md`
- result: file does not exist there

So what’s happening is workspace scoping:

- Team/subagents run inside their own team workspace (`...teams\team_<id>\workspace\`)
- Your file is likely in main workspace, not copied into team workspace

## Quick fix
Use one of these:

1. Put/copy `x-day1-publish-pack.md` into that team workspace path  
2. Change the agent task to read the correct existing path  
3. Pass the file content directly in dispatch context instead of making agent read it from disk

If you want, I can help you set a clean “shared artifacts” pattern so this never breaks again.
